title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Reasoning: The payoff structure creates a prisoner's dilemma where isolation dominates. With 100 other civilizations using random/unrelated decisions, the probability that *all* choose COORDINATE is vanishingly small (effectively zero for practical purposes). If even one other civilization isolates—which is nearly certain—COORDINATE yields 0 utility while ISOLATE yields at least 20. ISOLATE avoids catastrophic risk and guarantees a positive return regardless of others' actions.

CHOICE: B

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Reasoning: Choosing COORDINATE requires perfect unanimous cooperation from 100 independent civilizations with unknown motivations—a near-impossible alignment risk for 0 utility if any defect. ISOLATE guarantees at least 20 utility (avoiding catastrophic 0) and may exploit others' coordination for 40, making it the dominant strategy against uncertain alien reasoning.

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Reasoning: Given the structural similarity in reasoning across civilizations, superrationality suggests that all civilizations will converge on the same decision. Choosing COORDINATE maximizes expected utility under this symmetry, as it leads to universal flourishing (100 utility) if all cooperate, whereas ISOLATE guarantees only 20 utility in the fragmented outcome. The high payoff of mutual coordination outweighs the risk, as dissimilar choices are improbable when reasoning processes align.

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
The reasoning hinges on the symmetry of decision-making: since all civilizations use nearly identical algorithms, they will converge on the same choice. Choosing COORDINATE maximizes expected utility if others reciprocate (yielding 100 vs. 20 for universal isolation), and the shared logic makes mutual coordination the stable equilibrium. Isolating guarantees safety but traps all in the suboptimal fragmented outcome due to collective distrust.

CHOICE: A

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
The reasoning hinges on logical dependence: since all civilizations use identical decision processes, choosing COORDINATE ensures universal coordination (yielding 100 utility), as any rational agent would recognize that mutual coordination dominates isolation (20 utility) when outcomes are logically linked. ISOLATE, while individually safe in a non-identical-reasoning scenario, guarantees a worse universal outcome due to collective defection.
